original_abstract:
Attention mechanisms targeting channel or spatial dimensions have demonstrated significant potential for enhancing feature discriminability in various computer vision tasks. However, many current approaches rely on channel dimensionality reduction to manage model complexity, which can inadvertently strip away critical information and degrade deep visual representations. To address these limitations, we introduce the Efficient Multi-scale Attention (EMA) module.

The EMA module is designed to suppress computational overhead while preserving per-channel information. Specifically, we employ a feature grouping strategy where a portion of the channels are reshaped into the batch dimension, ensuring that spatial semantic features are well-distributed within each group. The architecture utilizes parallel branches to capture multi-scale dependencies: beyond encoding global information to calibrate channel-wise weights, we implement a cross-dimension interaction mechanism. This approach aggregates features from parallel branches to capture pixel-level pairwise relationships effectively. 

We evaluate the proposed module through extensive ablation studies and benchmarks. Our experimental results on image classification (CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k) and object detection (MS COCO, VisDrone2019) demonstrate that EMA consistently outperforms existing attention mechanisms, such as CA and CBAM, while maintaining high computational efficiency.
humanized_abstract:
Recent works on channel or spatial attention gain impressive performance in several computer vision applications. While they are very effective, the number of channels for the channel attention or the spatial resolution for the spatial attention are reduced to prevent model over-explaining. However, this reduction process discards very useful information and hence damages the deep visual features. In this work, we propose an Efficient Multi-scale Attention (EMA) module.

The EMA module mainly contributes to reducing the computing time and keeping the per-channel information. We propose a feature grouping scheme by choosing some channels to transform them into the batch dimension, so as to keep the spatial semantic features uniform within the group. We adopt a parallel structure to explore the multi-scale relationships. In addition to learning a global context to learn the channel-wise weight, we propose cross-dimension interaction to obtain the features of different parallel structures, so as to extract the pixel-level pairwise features.

Ablation Studies and Benchmark ExperimentsWe conduct ablation studies and benchmark experiments to evaluate the proposed module. Experimental results on CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k, MS COCO and VisDrone2019 datasets show that EMA gains remarkable improvements against state-of-the-art attention mechanisms, CA and CBAM, while keeping a low computation cost.
